title: SONiC Verified Troubleshooting Methodology
description: Five-phase troubleshooting process with built-in evidence verification at each step. Follow these phases in order using the specified MCP tools.

phases.name: DEFINE
phases.purpose: Establish a precise problem statement from the operator's symptom report.
instructions: Ask the operator: What is the symptom? When did it start? Which devices/interfaces/protocols are affected?
Search the KB for matching diagnostic trees and known failure patterns.
Identify the most specific diagnostic tree that matches the symptom.

tools.when: Find KB content matching the reported symptom
Load the decision tree for the symptom
Check if the symptom matches a known operator mistake

phases.exit_criteria: A diagnostic tree is identified and the entry symptom matches the operator's report.

phases.name: DIAGNOSE
phases.purpose: Walk the diagnostic tree with the operator, verifying each decision point.
instructions: Present the current branch question and the commands to run.
Wait for the operator to report the command output.
Follow the matching branch to the next node.
At each branch, note which code path step the verification command corresponds to (cross-reference).
Continue until reaching a leaf node (finding).

tools.when: Load a specific node with node_id parameter for resumption
Get additional verification commands for a protocol state
Look up a log message the operator reports seeing

phases.exit_criteria: A leaf node (finding) is reached with a specific diagnosis.

phases.name: EVIDENCE
phases.purpose: Build a cross-referenced evidence chain proving the diagnosis is correct.
instructions: Call explain_diagnosis with the tree_id and leaf node_id.
Review the evidence chain: does the code path support the diagnosis? Does the protocol spec agree?
If completeness gaps exist, acknowledge them to the operator.
Present the evidence chain to the operator before proceeding to the fix.

tools.when: ALWAYS call this after reaching a diagnostic leaf node
Get additional code path detail for the affected entity
Get full protocol documentation if more context is needed

phases.exit_criteria: Evidence chain is assembled and presented. The operator agrees the diagnosis is supported.

phases.name: RESOLVE
phases.purpose: Propose and verify the fix before the operator executes it.
instructions: Identify the fix from the diagnostic node's action field.
Call verify_action to trace what the fix command does under the hood.
Look up the relevant procedure if one exists.
Present the fix with: what it does, what DB tables it writes, what daemons process it, and how to verify it worked.
Include rollback steps from the procedure if available.

tools.when: ALWAYS call this before recommending a fix command
Look up a relevant operational procedure
Verify CONFIG_DB table and field names in the fix
Get details about daemons involved in processing the fix

phases.exit_criteria: Fix is verified against code paths and DB schemas. Rollback plan is available.

phases.name: VERIFY
phases.purpose: Confirm the fix resolved the original problem.
instructions: Use the verification_plan from the evidence chain.
Ask the operator to run each verification command.
Check that the symptom from Phase 1 is resolved.
Check that no new symptoms were introduced.
If the fix did not work, return to Phase 2 with the new state.

tools.when: Re-enter the diagnostic tree if the fix didn't work
Re-check the evidence if a different leaf is reached

phases.exit_criteria: All verification commands confirm the fix worked. Original symptom is resolved.

rules: NEVER recommend a fix without first calling verify_action to trace what it does.
NEVER skip the EVIDENCE phase -- always call explain_diagnosis after reaching a finding.
If the evidence chain has completeness gaps, state them explicitly to the operator.
All verification commands come from the KB, not from LLM generation.
If a diagnostic tree does not exist for the symptom, state that the KB does not cover this scenario.
Present evidence deterministically: same diagnosis should always show the same evidence chain.
